results.title: Way to Wimbledon
results.creator_credit: Franklin Gollings and Pathe Documentary Unit
results.brief_description:
Goes behind the scenes of the Wimbledon Tennis Championships and shows the care of the courts and the many and varied preparations for the tournaments.
Narrated by John Mills.

results.title: The Way to independence
results.brief_description: An allegory in three parts illustrating the transition of children from dependence to independence from parental control. Over-protective, indifferent and ideal parents are shown as their child takes the vital steps “into the world”. The viewer is left to draw their own conclusions.

results.title: Waters of time
results.brief_description: Produced for the Festival of Britain, this film combines a series of impressions of life along the 69 miles of the Thames from the mouth of the river to Kingston. The camera explores the wharves and warehouses of the waterfront; the docking of a ship; loading and reloading of its cargo; aerial views of the port; busy activity in the holds of the ships; wine cellars in warehouse basements. In the factual passages of the film the commentary is spoken by a dockmaster and docker; in the remainder of the film, the commentary is in prose and verse.

results.title: Watercolor painting: imaginative designs with Alex Ross
results.brief_description: Brilliant patterns of high-level color and semi-abstract forms emerge as the celebrated illustrator-painter demonstrates his work in his studio, and also shows his use of acrylic paint on a board producing compositions with an emotional dream-like quality.

results.title: Water: our national asset
results.creator_credit: B.F.C. Barrington and State Rivers and Water Supply Commission
results.brief_description: Shows the selection, development, maintenance and operation of irrigation schemes. Emphasises the importance of irrigation in keeping drought in check and increasing primary production.

results.title: Water movement in soils
results.creator_credit: J.C. Haish, W.E. Gardner and Washington State University
results.brief_description: The demonstration, using a model of a vertical section through the soil and time lapse photography, shows how liquid moves through soil of various compositions. The elements of the soil, which control the movement of water and liquid fertiliser, are of major importance in agriculture, irrigation, erosion control etc.
results.creator_credit_for_label: J.C. Haish, W.E. Gardner and Washington State University

results.title: The Water famine
results.creator_credit: Jack Beck and Columbia Broadcasting System Television
results.brief_description: The role of water in the birth and decay of civilization and the problems of man as he searches for new sources. The critical water shortage and the population explosion; the problems of pollution; desalinization demonstrated by the “flash freezing” process used in Israel and the electro-dialysis process used in the United States.

results.title: The Water cycle
results.creator_credit: Philip McDonald, Ron Bowie and New Zealand National Film Unit
results.brief_description: Illustrates how water is artificially trapped for power generation as well as a water supply for domestic, rural and industrial purposes. Dangers of pollution are emphasised, particularly the discharge of raw sewage into rivers and the sea.
